candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above EBIT, but it is a company-wide total and not specifically identified as relating to Materials and Services / COGS. Adding it to COGS would risk misclassification and double counting.
Separate operating depreciation line above EBIT, but not labelled as COGS-specific and appears to be part of the broader total D&A block rather than a cost-of-sales-only amount.
Separate operating amortisation line above EBIT, but not labelled as COGS-specific and likely part of the overall operating D&A block.
Residual component of the D&A block, not identified as COGS-related and too ambiguous to add to the selected COGS bucket.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is 'Materials and Services'. The excerpt shows only total operating D&A lines at the same level as other operating expenses, with no explicit indication of what portion belongs to COGS. To avoid double counting or forcing all operating D&A into COGS, no row is selected.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above EBIT and outside the selected XSGA rows ('Other operating expenses' and 'Employee expenses'), but it is a company-wide total and not specifically SG&A-only. It may also include non-SG&A elements, so adding it directly to XSGA would be unsafe.
Separate depreciation component of the total D&A block, but not labelled as SG&A-specific and may include amounts outside the selected XSGA bucket.

Separate amortisation component of the total D&A block, but not labelled as SG&A-specific and may include amounts outside the selected XSGA bucket, including possible R&D-related or other operating amounts.
Residual D&A component with unclear bucket attribution; too ambiguous to add to XSGA separately.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA reference bucket is the combination of 'Other operating expenses' and 'Employee expenses'. The statement presents D&A as a separate operating block, which suggests it is not embedded in those selected rows, but there is no SG&A-specific split. Because the visible D&A rows are company-wide rather than clearly attributable to SG&A, no row is selected to avoid misallocation and double counting.
